mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) captures underlying labor demand momentum; positive payroll growth signals continued economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls expanded to 143048k in Feb 2021 after pandemic lows
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint binds and forces investors to chase yield into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered observations, or mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales (RSAFS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signals robust consumer demand and labor market health; when both move favorably, earnings expectations revise upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE exhibits a t-statistic below 1.5 in predictive regressions, or the joint signal does not outperform the single-series RSAFS signal by at least 2pp in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns during the post-pandemic recovery phase because supply-side reopening directly boosts corporate revenues; outside of sharp rebound periods, this link weakens.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO recovering from pandemic lows as of early 2021
failure_condition: During identified post-shock recovery windows, INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or the signal underperforms a naive long benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE jumped from 3.5% to 14.7% during March-April 2020 before declining to 6.2% by Feb 2021
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the Sharpe ratio of the short signal is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a negative real rate environment that inflates equity valuations; this interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative in early 2021
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS level shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.10) in a 60-day forward regression, or the signal fails to beat a univariate CPIAUCSL model.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand that precedes downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when RSAFS declines, or the rank correlation between RSAFS changes and 20-day returns is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stage of a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reduces the present value of future cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month after a period of 12+ months unchanged or declining
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held near zero from March 2020 through Feb 2021
failure_condition: The first FEDFUNDS hike in a cycle fails to coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of historical episodes, or mean forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).
